missions.title: Hello, Container!
missions.story: Welcome, recruit. Your first mission is simple: prove you can launch a container. The docks are quiet, but not for long.
objectives: Run an `nginx` container in the foreground (any name)
hints: The command to run a container is `docker run`
Try `docker run nginx`
`docker run nginx` — this runs nginx in the foreground

missions.title: Detach and Name
missions.story: Running in the foreground blocks your terminal. Real operators run containers in the background. Name your assets so you can find them later.
objectives: Run an `nginx` container named `webserver` in detached mode
hints: Use flags to detach and name: `-d` and `--name`
`docker run -d --name webserver nginx`
`docker run -d --name webserver nginx`

missions.title: Inspect the Fleet
missions.story: A good operator always knows the state of their fleet. List all running containers and identify the one named `mission-agent`.
objectives: Run `docker ps` to see running containers (validation auto-passes after you run `docker ps` once)
Extra: Find the container ID of `mission-agent` and run `docker inspect mission-agent`
hints: `docker ps` lists running containers
`docker inspect <name>` shows full details
`docker ps` then `docker inspect mission-agent`

missions.title: Port Mapping
missions.story: The web server is running but no one outside can reach it. Map port 8080 on the host to port 80 inside the container.
objectives: Run an `nginx` container named `webserver` mapping host port 8080 to container port 80, in detached mode
hints: Use `-p` flag for port mapping: `-p host:container`
`docker run -d --name webserver -p 8080:80 nginx`
`docker run -d --name webserver -p 8080:80 nginx`

missions.title: Environment Variables
missions.story: Applications need configuration. Pass secrets and settings through environment variables — never hardcode them.
objectives: Run a container from image `alpine` named `env-demo` with env var `APP_ENV=production` and `APP_VERSION=1.0`, keep it running (use `sleep 3600`)
hints: Use `-e` flag to set environment variables
`docker run -d --name env-demo -e APP_ENV=production -e APP_VERSION=1.0 alpine sleep 3600`
`docker run -d --name env-demo -e APP_ENV=production -e APP_VERSION=1.0 alpine sleep 3600`

missions.title: Stop and Remove
missions.story: Containers left running waste resources. Stop and remove the container named `old-service` to clean up the docks.
hints: `docker stop <name>` stops a container; `docker rm <name>` removes it
Stop first, then remove: `docker stop old-service && docker rm old-service`
Or force remove in one step: `docker rm -f old-service`

missions.title: Exec Into a Container
missions.story: Something is wrong inside the `debug-box` container. Get a shell inside it and create a file `/tmp/mission-complete` to signal success.
objectives: Exec into container `debug-box` and create file `/tmp/mission-complete`
hints: `docker exec -it <name> <command>` runs a command inside a running container
Use `docker exec debug-box touch /tmp/mission-complete` or exec a shell
`docker exec debug-box touch /tmp/mission-complete`

missions.title: Container Logs
missions.story: The `log-service` container is misbehaving. Read its logs to find the secret code it printed, then create a file `/tmp/found` inside it to confirm you found it.
hints: `docker logs <name>` prints the stdout/stderr of a container
After reading logs, exec in and create the file
`docker logs log-service` then `docker exec log-service touch /tmp/found`

initialState.summary: - Container `log-service` (alpine, running) pre-created, it ran: `echo "SECRET_CODE=DOCKER42"` then `sleep 3600`
